attractions.name: Dalton Highway
attractions.season: Open year-round; practical driving window is Jun-Sep, winter is an ice-road proposition
attractions.cost: Free (fuel, tires and a rental that allows it are the real cost)
attractions.blurb: 414 mi of mostly gravel from Livengood to Deadhorse beside the pipeline; only three fuel stops the whole way.
attractions.tip: Most rental contracts ban it - use GoNorth, Alaska Auto Rental or Arctic Outfitters, and carry two spares.

attractions.name: E. L. Patton Yukon River Bridge & Yukon River Camp
attractions.season: Bridge open year-round; camp/cafe roughly late May-mid Sep
attractions.blurb: Alaska's only bridge over the Yukon - 2,295 ft of wooden deck that also carries the trans-Alaska pipeline.
attractions.tip: Mile 56 is the last fuel for 119 mi to Coldfoot. Camp has gas, a cafe and basic rooms; fill up regardless.

attractions.name: Finger Mountain Wayside
attractions.season: Snow-free and walkable Jun-Sep; buried and windswept in winter
attractions.blurb: BLM wayside among granite tors with a short tundra walk, interpretive panels and a toilet.
attractions.tip: Named for the finger-shaped rock on the ridge. Blueberries ripen here in late Aug; best light is evening.

attractions.name: Arctic Circle Wayside
attractions.season: Reachable year-round when the road is open; facilities summer only
attractions.blurb: The four-panel Arctic Circle sign and viewing deck at Dalton Mile 115 - the photo most Dalton trips are built on.
attractions.tip: Free Arctic Circle certificate at the Coldfoot visitor center. Campground 0.7 mi east: 5 sites, no fee, no water.
attractions.duration_note: 1 hr stop; ~5-6 hrs drive from Fairbanks

attractions.name: Prospect Creek
attractions.aka: Coldest place in US history, Dalton Mile 135
attractions.season: Visible whenever the road is open
attractions.blurb: Site of the -80F reading on 23 Jan 1971 - still the coldest temperature ever recorded in the United States.
attractions.tip: The pipeline construction camp is long gone; what's left is an airstrip and a sign. Two minutes, then drive on.

attractions.name: Coldfoot Camp
attractions.season: Open year-round; busiest Jun-Aug and during aurora season Sep-Mar
attractions.blurb: Old trucker camp at Mile 175: fuel, a 24-hour cafe and pipe-frame motel rooms. Last services for 240 mi north.
attractions.tip: Book rooms weeks ahead Jun-Aug. The buffet runs all night for truckers. Top off the tank here no matter what.

attractions.name: Arctic Interagency Visitor Center
attractions.season: Late May-early Sep, roughly 11am-10pm daily; closed in winter (limited BLM contact station)
attractions.blurb: Joint BLM/NPS/USFWS center at Coldfoot with Brooks Range exhibits, trip planning and a nightly 8pm ranger talk.
attractions.tip: The only place on the road to get real Gates of the Arctic backcountry info and Arctic Circle certificates.

attractions.name: Coldfoot Airport flightseeing base
attractions.season: Scheduled and charter flights Jun-Sep; charter only in winter
attractions.cost: ~$250-400 per seat for a 1-2 hr flightsee
attractions.blurb: Gravel strip beside Coldfoot Camp: the air-taxi and flightseeing base for Gates of the Arctic and Anaktuvuk Pass.
attractions.tip: Coyote Air and Wright Air operate here. A Brooks Range flightsee is the cheapest way to actually see the park.

attractions.name: Marion Creek Campground
attractions.season: Open roughly early Jun-mid Sep; water shut off after mid-Sep
attractions.blurb: BLM campground with 27 sites, potable water and a ~4 mi trail up to Marion Creek Falls in the Brooks foothills.
attractions.tip: First-come, first-served and the only developed campground north of the Yukon. Best tent option near Coldfoot.

attractions.name: Wiseman
attractions.season: Year-round; the 3 mi spur road can drift shut in winter
attractions.blurb: 1908 gold camp 3 mi off the Dalton with about a dozen residents, log cabins and the old Wiseman Trading Co.
attractions.tip: Turn at Mile 188.5. Boreal Lodge and Arctic Getaway rent cabins - far quieter and darker than Coldfoot.

attractions.name: Aurora over the Brooks Range at Wiseman
attractions.season: Late Aug-mid Apr; no aurora late May-late Jul because of the midnight sun
attractions.blurb: Wiseman sits directly under the auroral oval with zero light pollution - among the best aurora odds in Alaska.
attractions.tip: Give it 3 nights: clear-sky odds beat Fairbanks but a single cloudy night can wipe out a whole trip.

attractions.name: Sukakpak Mountain
attractions.season: Visible whenever the road is open; snow-free approach Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: A 4,459 ft marble fin tilted straight up off the highway at Mile 203 - the classic Brooks Range photograph.
attractions.tip: Best light is evening from the pullout just north. There is no trail; the approach is boggy tussock and palsas.

attractions.name: Atigun Pass
attractions.season: Kept open all year but closes for storms and avalanche control, especially Mar-May
attractions.blurb: At 4,739 ft, Alaska's highest year-round pass: the Continental Divide, the tree line and the Arctic watershed.
attractions.tip: Check Alaska 511 before committing; avalanche closures are common. Scan the walls for Dall sheep at the summit.

attractions.name: Atigun Gorge
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly mid Jun-mid Sep
attractions.blurb: Where the Atigun River cuts east out of the Brooks Range - the standard north-slope day hike and packraft put-in.
attractions.tip: Access on a rough spur off the Dalton near Mile 270. Tussock walking is brutal; 1 mph is a realistic pace.

attractions.name: Galbraith Lake Campground
attractions.season: Usable roughly Jun-Sep; unmaintained the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: Undeveloped BLM camping area 2.5 mi off the highway, ringed by the north wall of the Brooks Range.
attractions.tip: About 12 sites, vault toilet, no potable water. Wind is relentless - a freestanding tent with real stakes only.

attractions.name: Franklin Bluffs muskox & caribou flats
attractions.season: Muskox present year-round; Central Arctic caribou concentrate Jun-Jul
attractions.blurb: Ochre bluffs above the Sagavanirktok flats - the most reliable roadside muskox and caribou stretch on the Dalton.
attractions.tip: Scan with binoculars from the road; muskox look like black boulders that slowly move. Drive it slowly, both ways.

attractions.name: Deadhorse
attractions.season: Year-round; daily jet service from Anchorage and Fairbanks
attractions.blurb: End of the Dalton at Mile 414: an industrial oilfield camp of gravel pads, worker hotels and no town at all.
attractions.tip: No bar, no shops, and you cannot reach the ocean on your own. Book a bed and the ocean shuttle before you leave.
attractions.duration_note: one night before flying out or turning around

attractions.name: Arctic Ocean Shuttle & Arctic Ocean dip
attractions.season: Runs roughly Jun-Sep when oilfield road and shore access allow
attractions.blurb: Escorted bus through the restricted Prudhoe oilfield to the Beaufort Sea shore - the only legal way to the water.
attractions.tip: Reserve 24+ hrs ahead with photo ID or passport for security clearance. Bring a towel if you plan to go in.

attractions.name: Gates of the Arctic National Park & Preserve
attractions.season: Backcountry season roughly mid Jun-early Sep; mosquitoes peak late Jun-Jul
attractions.blurb: 8.4M acres of Brooks Range with no roads, trails, campgrounds or signs - the least-visited US national park.
attractions.tip: No fee and no permit, but you must fly in. Free bear-canister loans and route advice at Bettles or Coldfoot.

attractions.name: The Gates: Boreal Mountain & Frigid Crags
attractions.season: Flyable Jun-Sep; river floats start after ice-out in mid Jun
attractions.blurb: The twin portals on the North Fork Koyukuk that Bob Marshall named the gates of the Arctic in 1929.
attractions.tip: Seen on a Bettles flightseeing loop, or on foot via a float-plane drop at Redstar Lake plus a river float.

attractions.name: Arrigetch Peaks
attractions.season: Mid Jun-early Sep only; bugs worst in Jul, weather turns by early Sep
attractions.blurb: Granite spires above the Alatna River - Gates of the Arctic's signature backpacking and big-wall objective.
attractions.tip: Float-plane to Circle Lake, then two hard off-trail days up Arrigetch Creek. NPS asks parties to camp low.

attractions.name: Bettles
attractions.season: Year-round air service; visitor season Jun-Sep plus aurora Feb-Mar
attractions.blurb: Tiny river village and gravel strip on the Koyukuk that serves as the main air gateway into Gates of the Arctic.
attractions.tip: Wright Air flies from Fairbanks in ~45 min. The NPS ranger station here handles route planning and bear cans.

attractions.name: Anaktuvuk Pass
attractions.season: Year-round flights; caribou migrations pass Apr-May and Aug-Sep
attractions.blurb: Nunamiut Inupiat village of ~330 at 2,200 ft inside Gates of the Arctic - the last inland caribou-hunting settlement.
attractions.tip: Daily Wright Air service from Fairbanks. Much of the land around town is Native corporation owned - ask first.

attractions.name: Simon Paneak Memorial Museum
attractions.season: Open year-round on weekdays; staffing is thin, so call ahead
attractions.blurb: Village museum on Nunamiut life: caribou-skin masks, sod-house models and the 1950s shift from nomadic hunting.
attractions.tip: Caribou-skin masks are still made in the village and sold here - one of the few places you can buy them.

attractions.name: Iniakuk Lake Wilderness Lodge
attractions.season: Summer season Jun-Sep; limited aurora dates in Mar
attractions.blurb: Float-plane-only log lodge on a lake at the edge of Gates of the Arctic; the only full-service lodge in the area.
attractions.tip: Rates include the Fairbanks flight and guided day trips; three-night minimum and it books out a year ahead.

attractions.name: Kobuk Valley National Park
attractions.season: Charter season Jun-Sep; caribou cross the Kobuk late Aug-Sep
attractions.blurb: 1.7M roadless acres straddling the Kobuk River - no trails, no facilities, one of the least-visited US parks.
attractions.tip: Most visitors do a single day landing on the dunes from Kotzebue. No permit or fee, but file a plan with someone.

attractions.name: Great Kobuk Sand Dunes
attractions.season: Landable roughly Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: 25 sq mi of active dunes up to 100 ft high above the Arctic tree line - relics of Pleistocene glacial outwash.
attractions.tip: Tundra-tire bush planes land right on the sand. On a clear July day the sand surface can exceed 100F.

attractions.name: Noatak National Preserve
attractions.season: Float season after ice-out, roughly late Jun-early Sep
attractions.cost: Free entry; ~$1,500+ air taxi plus raft rental
attractions.blurb: 6.6M acres protecting the whole Noatak River basin - the largest essentially untouched river basin in North America.
attractions.tip: Classic trip is a 10-14 day Class I-II float from Lake Matcharak or the Cutler River down to Noatak village.

attractions.name: Cape Krusenstern National Monument
attractions.season: Boat/plane access Jun-Sep; snowmachine in spring
attractions.blurb: 114 beach ridges on the Chukchi coast recording more than 5,000 years of Inupiat sea-mammal hunting camps.
attractions.tip: No facilities of any kind, ~20 min flight from Kotzebue, and much of the land inside is Native-owned - ask first.

attractions.name: Kotzebue
attractions.season: Year-round; visitor season Jun-Sep, midnight sun early Jun-early Aug
attractions.blurb: Inupiat hub of ~3,100 on a gravel spit 26 mi above the Arctic Circle; gateway to Kobuk, Noatak and Cape Krusenstern.
attractions.tip: Daily Alaska Airlines jets from Anchorage. In late Jun the sun sets over the Chukchi around 2am and rises at 4.

attractions.name: Northwest Arctic Heritage Center
attractions.season: Mon-Fri 8-5 mid Mar-mid Nov; Mon-Fri 10:30-4:30 mid Nov-mid Mar; closed weekends
attractions.blurb: NPS center in Kotzebue with Inupiat subsistence exhibits and the planning desk for three roadless Arctic parks.
attractions.tip: Closed Saturday and Sunday - schedule your Kotzebue flights around a weekday if you want park briefings.

attractions.name: Nome
attractions.season: Year-round; road season Jun-Sep, Iditarod finish in mid Mar
attractions.blurb: Gold-rush town of ~3,500 on the Bering Sea with no road to the rest of Alaska but 250 mi of its own gravel roads.
attractions.tip: Daily jets from Anchorage (~1h45). Reserve a rental truck months out - Nome has only a few dozen vehicles.
attractions.duration_note: 3-4 days to drive all three roads

attractions.name: Iditarod finish line & Burled Arch
attractions.season: Race arrives mid-March; the arch itself is on display in town year-round
attractions.blurb: Front Street finish of the 1,000-mile Iditarod, under a spruce burled arch raised each March.
attractions.tip: The winner usually arrives 8-10 days after the Anchorage restart, about Mar 10-13. Book flights 6+ months out.

attractions.name: Nome gold beaches
attractions.season: Ice-free roughly mid Jun-early Oct
attractions.cost: Free; rent a pan or sluice in town
attractions.blurb: Public recreational-mining beach where anyone can pan or sluice Bering Sea gold with no claim or permit.
attractions.tip: Hand tools and small dredges only, and stay inside the posted public stretch - commercial claims start past it.

attractions.name: Carrie M. McLain Memorial Museum
attractions.season: Open year-round; closed Sundays and Mondays
attractions.blurb: Nome's museum covering the 1899 gold rush, Bering Strait Inupiat culture and Iditarod history.
attractions.tip: Shares the Richard Foster Building with the library and the Katirvik Cultural Center - do both in one stop.

attractions.name: Anvil Mountain White Alice antennas
attractions.season: Road usually drivable Jun-Sep; snow-covered in winter
attractions.blurb: Four four-story parabolic Cold War tropospheric-scatter antennas on the ridge above Nome, listed on the NRHP.
attractions.tip: Fifteen minutes from town up Anvil Mountain Road. Go near midnight in June - the light and the view are the point.

attractions.name: Nome-Council Road & the Last Train to Nowhere
attractions.season: Maintained Jun-Sep; not plowed to the end in winter
attractions.blurb: 72 mi of gravel east along the Bering coast; at Mile 33 three 1881 locomotives rust into the tundra at Solomon.
attractions.tip: The engines came from the New York elevated railway. Road ends at the Niukluk River - no bridge across to Council.

attractions.name: Safety Sound
attractions.season: Birding peak late May-Jun; frozen and empty Nov-May
attractions.blurb: Shallow lagoon at Council Road Mile 17-22 - one of Alaska's best roadside spots for Arctic-breeding birds.
attractions.tip: Late May-mid Jun for king and spectacled eider, loons and jaegers. Scope from the pullouts; do not walk the spit.

attractions.name: Bob Blodgett Nome-Teller Highway
attractions.season: Maintained Jun-Sep; drifted shut in winter
attractions.blurb: 73 mi northwest over open tundra to Teller, an Inupiat village of ~230 on a spit between harbor and open sea.
attractions.tip: The best muskox road out of Nome - scan ridgelines and hilltops. Zero services: fuel, food and a spare tire.

attractions.name: Kougarok Road & Salmon Lake
attractions.season: Passable roughly mid Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: Runs 86 mi north into the Kigluaik Mountains; free BLM campground and grayling fishing at Salmon Lake, Mile 38.
attractions.tip: Mile 72 Coffee Dome is the classic hike for bristle-thighed curlew - the only place in the US you can reach them.

attractions.name: Pilgrim Hot Springs
attractions.season: Road access roughly mid Jun-Sep; snowmachine in winter
attractions.blurb: Geothermal springs 60 mi from Nome with a soaking tub beside the ruins of a 1918 Catholic mission and orphanage.
attractions.tip: Free permit required from the Nome Visitor Center. Turn at Kougarok Mile 53 then 7 mi of rough 4WD-only spur.

attractions.name: Bering Land Bridge National Preserve
attractions.season: Air charter Jun-Sep; snowmachine access Mar-Apr
attractions.blurb: 2.7M acres of what's left of Beringia: maar lakes, lava flows and tundra, with no roads, trails or facilities.
attractions.tip: Nothing is staffed inside the preserve - the office is on Front Street in Nome. Charter from Nome or Kotzebue.

attractions.name: Serpentine Hot Springs
attractions.season: Fly-in Jun-Sep; snowmachine or dogsled Feb-Apr
attractions.cost: Free to use; ~$1,000 charter from Nome
attractions.blurb: Granite-tor basin inside Bering Land Bridge with a soaking tub in a shed and a free 12-bunk NPS bunkhouse.
attractions.tip: Bunkhouse is first-come, no reservations. Unmaintained gravel strip a short walk away; bring all your own food.

attractions.name: Utqiagvik
attractions.season: Year-round; sun never sets ~11 May-2 Aug, never rises ~18 Nov-23 Jan
attractions.blurb: Northernmost town in the US: ~4,900 mostly Inupiat residents on the Chukchi coast, 320 mi above the Arctic Circle.
attractions.tip: Daily Alaska Airlines jet via Fairbanks. Alcohol sales are banned and everything is flown in - budget accordingly.

attractions.name: Inupiat Heritage Center
attractions.season: Year-round: Mon-Fri 8:30am-5pm, Sat-Sun 11am-3pm
attractions.blurb: Utqiagvik museum affiliated with New Bedford Whaling NHP: bowhead whaling, umiaks, artifacts and craft demos.
attractions.tip: The traditional room usually has carvers or skin sewers working - the best part and easy to walk past.

attractions.name: Point Barrow
attractions.season: Tundra-vehicle tours Jun-Sep; polar bears most likely Sep-Oct
attractions.blurb: The northernmost point of the United States, 12 mi past town, where the Chukchi and Beaufort seas meet.
attractions.tip: Guided 4x4 only. The whale bone pile out here draws polar bears - never walk or drive the spit on your own.

attractions.name: Utqiagvik whale bone arch
attractions.season: Year-round
attractions.blurb: Bowhead jawbone arch on the Chukchi beach beside hauled-up umiaks - Utqiagvik's standard photograph.
attractions.tip: On the beachfront a few minutes' walk from the Top of the World Hotel. Sea ice can still be offshore in June.

attractions.name: Nalukataq
attractions.season: Late June only, after the spring bowhead hunt; exact dates set locally
attractions.cost: Free; visitors welcome (bring a bowl and a bag)
attractions.blurb: Inupiat spring-whaling festival where successful crews share muktuk with the whole town, ending in a blanket toss.
attractions.tip: Usually falls in the last ten days of June but depends on the hunt - confirm with the borough before booking.

attractions.name: Will Rogers & Wiley Post Memorial
attractions.season: Reachable by tundra vehicle roughly Jun-Sep
attractions.cost: Usually included in a town tour
attractions.blurb: Monument at the 1935 crash site ~15 mi south of Utqiagvik where humorist Will Rogers and pilot Wiley Post died.
attractions.tip: Tundra track only - go with a local guide or ATV tour. A second, easier marker stands near the airport in town.

attractions.name: Kaktovik polar bear viewing
attractions.season: Bears ashore Aug-Oct; boat tours end when the lagoon freezes, usually late Oct
attractions.blurb: Inupiat village on Barter Island where polar bears gather each fall on barrier islands and the whale bone pile.
attractions.tip: Peak is early Sep-mid Oct. Flights cancel constantly for weather - build in two buffer days or you will miss it.

attractions.name: Arctic National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.season: Trip season mid Jun-early Sep; Porcupine caribou calve on the coastal plain in June
attractions.cost: Free entry; ~$5,000+ for a guided float
attractions.blurb: 19.6M acres from the Brooks Range crest to the Beaufort coast - calving ground of the Porcupine caribou herd.
attractions.tip: No roads, trails, campgrounds or signs. Bush flights from Fairbanks or Coldfoot; guided trips book a year out.
